Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 073 [Rituals to obtain an oracular decision][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

[EN₂ il₃-tu₄] rem₂-ni-tu₄ mu-bal-li-ṭa-at lu₂UŠ₂

(o i 1) [Incantation:] O merciful [goddess], reviver of the dying man,

o i 22

[ša₂ nap]-⸢lu⸣-[us-sa] ba-la-ṭu u na-as₂-ḫur-ša₂ ša₂-la-⸢mu⸣

(o i 2) [whose] look is life and whose favourable attention is peace;

o i 33

[ap-kal-lat] ⸢DINGIR⸣-MEŠ ga-me-rat ab-ra-a-[ti]

(o i 3) [wise woman] among the gods, controller of the populace,

o i 44

[ṣa-bi]-⸢ta⸣-at₂ mar-⸢kas kip⸣-pat ANe u KIti₃

(o i 4) holder of the rope of the circumference of heaven and earth,

o i 55

[mu]-⸢kil-lat⸣ mar-ka-⸢si⸣ GALi ša E₂.ŠAR₂.RA

(o i 5) holder of the great rope of Ešara.

o i 66

ina ANe šu-tur KI.GUB-ki ina ⸢d⸣iš₈-tar₂-MEŠ ša₂-qa-a re-ša₂-a-ki

(o i 6) Your position is supreme in heaven; your head is highest among the goddesses.

o i 77

da-nu dBAD u de₂-⸢a⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ AD!(GAL-)MEŠ-ki

(o i 7) Anu, Ellil and Ea, the gods, your fathers, cannot establish an order without you.

o i 88

[ina ba]-⸢li-ki⸣ ul i-ša₂-⸢ka⸣-nu ur-⸢ta⸣

o i 99

[ba-ni-tu₂ ṣir-tu₂] ša₂ e-la ša₂-ša₂ ⸢la⸣ DU₃a ab-ra-a-ti

(o i 9) [Exalted creator], without whom the populace cannot be created

o i 1010

[u₃ EŠ.BAR] ANe u KIti la₃ KU₅su

(o i 10) [and the decision(s)] of heaven and earth cannot be decided.

o i 1111

[ana-ku al]-⸢si⸣-ki du-uš-mu-u₂ pa-liḫ-⸢ki⸣

(o i 11) [I], (your) servant, the one who reveres you, called out to you.

o i 1212

[ana NIG₂.GIG] ⸢im⸣-ḫur-an-⸢ni⸣-ma ⸢ina šap⸣-li-ki ak-mi-su

(o i 12) I knelt beneath you [on account of the evil] happening to me.

o i 1313

[ṭe₃-em DINGIR]ti-ki GAL⸢ti⸣ aš₂-šu lib-luṭ

(o i 13) [(Concerning) the decision] of your great divinity that he may recover, [you were merciful] to me and you looked after me.

o i 1414

[ta-re-min₃]-⸢ni⸣-ma ta-ṭe₃-min₃-ni

o i 1515

[ki-i] ⸢NENNI⸣ A NENNI GIG ⸢an⸣-na-a ša₂ mar-ṣu

(o i 15) [If] you would make so-and-so, son of so-and-so, this patient who is sick, stand up [from] this [illness], you should convey a message to the gods, your fathers, and the gods, your fathers, will pay attention [to] your exalted [command] and [...] ... they will spare him and allow him to tread the streets of his city healthily.

o i 1616

[ina GIG an]-⸢ni⸣-i ⸢tu!(šu-)šat-bi-i ana DINGIR-MEŠ AD!(GAL-)MEŠ-ki a-bu-su ta-ṣab-ba-ti-ma

o i 1717

[ana qi₂-bi-ti]-⸢ki⸣ ṣir-ti DINGIR-MEŠ AD!(GAL-)MEŠ-ki i-qul-lu-ma

o i 1818

[...] x i-gam-me-lu-šu u SILA IRI-šu₂ ina SILIMme u₂-šak-ba-⸢su⸣

o i 1919

[ki-i ša₂] ta-re-min₃-ni-ma ṭe₃-em DINGIRti-ki GALti ta-ṭe₃-⸢min₃⸣-[ni]

(o i 19) [Just as] you were merciful to me (and) looked after me (regarding) the decision of your great divinity, send me the decision of your great divinity (so that) my mouth may make it known.

o i 2020

⸢ṭe₃-em DINGIRti-ki⸣ GALti šup-rim-ma KA-ia₅ lu-še-ṣi

o i 2121

EN₂ il₃-tu₄ rem₂-ni-tu₄ mu-bal-li-⸢ṭa⸣-at lu₂UŠ₂

(o i 21) Incantation: O merciful goddess, reviver of the dying man,

o i 2222

ša₂ nap-lu-us-sa ba-la-ṭu u na-as₂-ḫur-ša₂ ša₂-la-mu

(o i 22) whose look is life and whose favourable attention is peace;

o i 2323

ap-kal-lat DINGIR-MEŠ ga-me-⸢rat⸣ ab-ra-⸢a-ti⸣

(o i 23) wise woman among the gods, controller of the populace,

o i 2424

ṣa-bi-ta-at₂ mar-kas ⸢kip-pat⸣ <AN>⸢e⸣ u ⸢KIti₃⸣

(o i 24) holder of the rope of the circumference of heaven and earth,

o i 2525

mu-kil-lat mar-ka-si [GAL]i ša E₂.⸢ŠAR₂⸣.RA

(o i 25) holder of the [great] rope of Ešara.

o i 2626

ina ANe šu-tur KI.⸢GUB-ki⸣ ina diš₈-tar₂-MEŠ ša₂-qa-a re-ša₂-a-ki

(o i 26) Your position is supreme in heaven; your head is highest among the goddesses.

o i 2727

da-nu ⸢d⸣[BAD u] de₂-a DINGIR-MEŠ AD-MEŠ-ki

(o i 27) Anu, Ellil and Ea, the gods, your fathers, cannot establish an order without [you].

o i 2828

ina ba-[li-ki] ul i-ša₂-ka-nu ⸢ur-ta⸣

o i 2929

[ba]-⸢ni-tu₂ ṣir⸣-[tu₂ ša₂] ⸢e⸣-la ša₂-ša₂ la DU₃a ab-ra-a-⸢ti⸣

(o i 29) Exalted creator, without [whom] the populace cannot be created

o i 3030

u₃ EŠ.BAR ANe KIti la₃ KU₅⸢su⸣

(o i 30) and the decision(s) of heaven and earth cannot be decided.

o i 3131

ana-ku al-si-ki du-uš-mu-u₂ pa-liḫ-ki

(o i 31) I, (your) servant, the one who reveres you, called out to you.

o i 3232

ana NIG₂.GIG im-ḫur-an-ni-ma ina šap-li-ki ak-mi-su

(o i 32) I knelt before you on account of the evil happening to me.

o i 3333

ṭe₃-em DINGIRti-ki GALti aš₂-šu mi-⸢it⸣-ti

(o i 33) (Concerning) the decision of your great divinity about the dying man, you were merciful to me and you looked after me [:] If ... so-and-so, son of so-and-so, the patient who is sick, ... this illness.

o i 3434

ta-re-min₃-ni-ma ta-ṭe₃-min₃-ni [:] ki-i NENNI A NENNI GIG ša₂ mar-ṣu GIG an-⸢nam? NIN⸣ U₂

o i 3535

u₂-ru-uḫ-šu₂ ana KUR.NU.GI₄.A šu-uḫ-mu-ṭa-at

(o i 35) His path to the Land Of No Return is being hastened;

o i 3636

dGEŠʾU šu-ut ku-dur₂-ra ša₂ LIL₂ NENNI ik-di-ru u₂-ru-uḫ KUR.NU.GI₄.A na-šu-šu

(o i 36) the Anunnaku, those who delimit the boundary of so-and-so's sickness, are lifting the path to the Land Of No Return for him;

o i 3737

DINGIR-šu₂ u d15-šu u₄-um-šu u₃ šim-ta-šu

(o i 37) his god and his goddess released for him his day and his fate; he is taking to the road and standing on the path of no return.

o i 3838

u₂-maš-ši-⸢ru⸣-šu-ma ur-ḫa šu-te-šu-ra : ⸢u₃⸣ ḫar-ra-an la ta-ri te-bu-u₂

o i 3939

SILA IRI-šu₂ ⸢zu⸣-mu-ma a-na du-ur da-ri la i-kab-ba-su

(o i 39) He will be deprived of the streets of his city; for all eternity he will not tread them (again).

o i 4040

ki-i ša₂ ta-re-min₃-ni-ma ṭe₃-em DINGIRti-ki GALti ta-ṭe₃-min₃-ni

(o i 40) Just as you were merciful to me (and) looked after me (regarding) the decision of your great divinity, send me the decision of your great divinity (so that) my mouth may make it known.

o i 4141

ṭe₃-em DINGIRti-ki GALti šup-rim-ma KA-ia lu-še-ṣi

Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

KA.INIM.MA ⸢EŠ⸣.[BAR IGI.DU₈ DU₃.DU₃]-⸢BI a-na⸣ IGI [... GAR]⸢an⸣ KAŠ BALqi₂ 2 u₂-il₃-ti te-ʾi-⸢il⸣

(o ii 1) Incantation [to see] a decision. Its [ritual]: [you set up ...] before [...]. You libate beer (and) bind (it) with two contract-tablets.

o ii 22

ina IGI mul[...] x MU KIL DIŠ A₂ x AN x [...] ⸢ta⸣-šak-⸢kan⸣-ma EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈

(o ii 2) Before the [...] star you place ... [...] and you will see the decision.

o ii 33

⸢EN₂⸣ at-[tu]-⸢nu⸣ MUL-MEŠ ša₂ mu-ši-⸢ti dEN⸣.BI.LUḪ d⸢NIN⸣.[BI.LUḪ d]EN.BI.LUḪ.ḪA

(o ii 3) Incantation: O you, stars of the night, Enbiluh, Ninbiluh, Enbiluha, [and] the night, the veiled bride; Ea, king of the Apsu, support of Eridu, the Sebettu, the Seven, inhabitants of the heavens, pure [gods].

o ii 44

[u] mu-ši-tu₄ kal-la-tu₄ kut!(NU-)⸢tum-tu₄⸣ : de₂-a [MAN] ⸢ABZU⸣ tuk-lat eri-du₁₀

o ii 55

⸢d⸣7-⸢BI⸣ d7 a-šib ša₂-ma-[mi DINGIR]-⸢MEŠ⸣ KU₃-MEŠ ia-[a]-⸢ti ARAD⸣-ku-nu

(o ii 5) (As for) me, your servant, make me speak with my god and my goddess and : may they either make the judgement that I am weak or decide my verdict that I am strong.

o ii 66

⸢KI⸣ DINGIR-ia₅ d15-ia₅ šu-ud-[bi-ba]-⸢nin⸣-ni-ma : lu-u₂ en-še!(KUR-)ku di-ni ⸢li⸣-[di-nu] ⸢lu⸣-u₂ ⸢dan⸣-na ana-ku EŠ.BAR-a-a lip!-ru!-su

o ii 77

⸢KA.INIM⸣.MA EŠ.BAR ⸢MAŠ₂⸣.[GE₆ IGI.DU₈] DU₃.DU₃-BI ⸢ina⸣ GE₆ ana IGI MUL-MEŠ GE₆ti₃

(o ii 7) Incantation [to see] a decision (or) a dream. Its ritual: [you set up] a censer of [juniper] in the night, before the nightly stars (and) you recite this incantation three times over (a piece of) tamarisk.

o ii 88

[NIG₂].⸢NA šim⸣[LI GARan] ⸢EN₂⸣ an-ni-tu₄ 3-šu₂ ana UGU gišbi-ni ŠIDnu-ma

o ii 99

[...] GARan ina SAG gišNU₂-ka

(o ii 9) [...] you set up at the head of your bed;

o ii 1010

[... DINGIR ra-ma-ni] ša₂ ZI₃.MAD.GA₂ te-ṣir NU₂-ma EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈

(o ii 10) you draw [... of (your) personal god] of maṣhatu-flour (and) lie down, and you will see the decision.

o ii 1111

[EN₂ ra-am]-ku ša₂ de₂-a DUMU šip-ri ša₂ dASAR.LU₂.ḪI ana-ku DINGIR-MEŠ GE₆ti₃ ina? [...]

(o ii 11) [Incantation:] I am the bathed priest of Ea, messenger of Asalluhi. O gods of the night, in [...];

o ii 1212

[dEN.BI.LUḪ] ⸢dNIN⸣.BI.LUḪ DINGIR-MEŠ EN-MEŠ GE₆ti₃ mu-ši-tu₄ kal!(E-)la-tu₄ ⸢kut⸣-[tum-tu₄]

(o ii 12) [O Enbiluh], Ninbiluh, the gods, lords of the night, (and) the night, the veiled bride;

o ii 1313

⸢d⸣[e₂-a] MAN ABZU tuk-lat eri-du₁₀ d7-BI d7 a-šib ša₂-ma-mi DINGIR-MEŠ [KU₃-MEŠ]

(o ii 13) [Ea], the king of the Apsu, the support of Eridu; the Sebettu, the Seven, inhabitants of the heavens, pure gods.

o ii 1414

ia-[a-ti] ⸢ARAD⸣-ku-nu KI DINGIR-ia₅ d15-ia₅ šu-ud-bi-ba-nin-ni-ma lu-u en-še!(KUR-)ku di-ni li-di-nu EŠ.BAR-⸢a⸣-[a lip-ru-su]

(o ii 14) (As for) me, your servant, make me speak with my god (and) my goddess and : may they either make the judgement that I am weak (or) decide my verdict.

o ii 1515

KA.INIM.MA KI DINGIR-šu₂ u diš₈-tar₂-šu₂ da-ba-bi-im-ma EGIR NI₂-šu₂ pa-ra-si

(o ii 15) Incantation to speak with one's god and goddess and to determine one's future.

o ii 1616

DU₃.DU₃-⸢BI NI₂-ka⸣ DADAG ina!(TAB) SAG eb-bi u eb-be₂-ti GIŠ.ḪUR-MEŠ

(o ii 16) Its ritual: You cleanse yourself. At the heads of a pure man and a pure woman you make drawings of Ea and Asalluhi and you ... with your god and your goddess. They will decide your own future for you.

o ii 1717

ša₂ de₂-a u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI te-ṣir-ma KI DINGIR-ka u d15-ka

o ii 1818

ta-x x x x x ⸢EGIR⸣ NI₂-ka i-par-ra-su-ka

o ii 1919

GIŠ.⸢ḪUR⸣ [an-ni]-⸢tu₂⸣ ša₂ e-⸢ṣi-ru-ka⸣ te-ṣir

(o ii 19) You make this drawing that I drew for you.

o ii 2020

EN₂ mulMAR.GID₂.DA.⸢AN.NA MAR⸣.GID₂.⸢DA⸣ [ša₂-ma]-mi ša₂ ni-ir!(NI-)ša₂ dNIN.URTA

(o ii 20) Incantation: O Wagon of Heaven: the cart of the heavens, whose yoke is Ninurta, whose yoke-shaft is Marduk, whose axles are the daughters of Anu of the pure heavens, who is rising before Assur (and) whose face is turned towards Babylon.

o ii 2121

ma-šad-da-ša₂ dAMAR.[UTU bu]-⸢ba⸣-tu-ša₂ ⸢DUMU⸣.[MUNUS] ⸢d⸣a-nu₃ ša₂ ANe KU₃-MEŠ

o ii 2222

ana IGI aš+šurki ⸢nap-ḫa-at?⸣ ana IGI ⸢KA₂⸣.[DINGIR].⸢RA⸣ki IGI-ša₂ šak-nu

o ii 2323

ki-i NENNI A ⸢NENNI⸣ i-⸢bal-lu-ṭu⸣ i-⸢šal⸣-[li-mu] ⸢mu⸣-šu lib-lam!(KID-)ma MAŠ₂.GE₆ IGI

(o ii 23) May the night bring me (the decision) whether so-and-so, son of so-and-so, will live (and) be healthy. May I see a dream!

o ii 2424

KA.INIM.MA EŠ.⸢BAR⸣ IGI!(DU₃)[DU₈] DU₃.DU₃-BI ⸢še-im⸣ ša 1 ⸢ḫar⸣-bi TIqe₂ 1-TA.AM₃ te-bi-ir

(o ii 24) Incantation to see a decision. Its ritual: You take (enough) barley for 1 plough. You cross it once.

o ii 2525

ŠE BI GE₆ tu-na-[pa]-⸢ṣa? lu₂TUR⸣ ša₂ MUNUS la₃ ZUu ARA₃en ana IGI mulMAR.GID₂.DA

(o ii 25) At night you crush that barley and a child who does not know a woman, grinds (it). Before the Wagon, you sweep the roof; you sprinkle pure water to make a circle; you set up a censer of burāšu-juniper and some maṣhatu-flour (and) recite the incantation 3 times. You speak with nobody. You lie down and you will see a dream.

o ii 2626

UR₃ SAR ⸢A-MEŠ⸣ KU₃-⸢MEŠ⸣ SU₃ su-ur-ta tu-sa-ar NIG₂.NA šimLI u ZI₃.MAD.GA₂ GARan

o ii 2727

EN₂ 3-šu₂ ⸢ŠID⸣nu KI lu₂⸢NA⸣.ME la₃ DU₁₁.DU₁₁ub NU₂-ma MAŠ₂.GE₆ IGI

o ii 2828

šum-ma mim-ma SUM-šu₂ ⸢GIG TI⸣ šum-ma mim-ma la₃ SUM-šu₂ GIG UŠ₂

(o ii 28) If anything is given to him, the patient will recover; if nothing is given to him, the patient will die.

o ii 2929

šum-ma ana DU₃eš A₂.AŠ₂ DU₃⸢uš mim⸣-ma SUM-šu₂ AŠ₂-su KURad₂ la₃ SUM-šu₂ NU KURad₂

(o ii 29) If you perform (this ritual) to fulfil a wish: (if) anything is given to him, he will achieve his wish; (if) nothing is given to him, he will not achieve (it).

o ii 3030

EN₂ at!(MAR-)ti mulMAR.GID₂.⸢DA⸣.[AN].⸢NA⸣ ša₂ ANe KIti ni-ir-ki dNIN.URTA ma-šad-da-ki dAMAR.UTU

(o ii 30) Incantation: O you, Wagon of Heaven of heaven and earth: your yoke is Ninurta, your yoke-shaft is Marduk, your axles are the daughters of Anu of the pure heavens, you are rising in Assyria (and) your face is turned towards Babylon. Without you, the dying cannot die and the living cannot seize (their) path.

o ii 3131

[bu]-ba-tu-ki DUMU.MUNUS da-nu₃ ša₂ ANe KU₃-MEŠ ina KUR aš+šurki nap-ḫa-ti

o ii 3232

ina ⸢KA₂⸣.DINGIR.RAki IGI-ki GARnu ina ⸢ba⸣-li-ki lu₂UŠ₂ la₃ UŠ₂ u₃ TI ḫar-ra-an la₃ DABbat

o ii 3333

⸢šum-ma⸣ KASKAL te-ba-ku ⸢ṣi⸣-[bu]-⸢ti⸣ KURad₂ mim-ma lid-di-nu-ni

(o ii 33) If I am to achieve (my) wish on the journey I am starting, may they give me something.

o ii 3434

⸢šum-ma⸣ [KASKAL] ⸢te-ba⸣-ku ⸢ṣi⸣-[bu-ti] la₃ KURad₂ mim-ma lim-ḫu-ru-nin-ni

(o ii 34) If I am not to achieve (my) wish on the journey I am starting, may they receive something from me.

o ii 3535

[KA].⸢INIM⸣.MA ana ⸢A₂⸣.[AŠ₂] ⸢te⸣-bi EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈

(o ii 35) Incantation to see a decision on account of setting out (to achieve) a wish.

o ii 3636

⸢EN₂⸣ mulMAR.⸢GID₂.DA⸣ DUMU.⸢MUNUS⸣ [da]-⸢nu₃⸣ GALtu₄ kal-lat E₂.KUR ⸢kul-lul⸣-tu

(o ii 36) Incantation: O Wagon, eldest daughter of Anu, veiled bride of the Ekur, you are the decreer of destinies, the distributer of barley rations.

o ii 3737

mu-šim-⸢tu₄⸣ ša₂ NAM-[MEŠ mu-za-aʾ]-iz!(A₂-)tu ša₂ šeKURUM₆-MEŠ at-ti

o ii 3838

šum-ma mim-ma ki-⸢a⸣-[am ša₂] SIG₅-MEŠ ḪA.LA lid-di-nu-ni

(o ii 38) If (there is) thus to be anything [of] good fortune, let them give me a share!

o ii 3939

šum-ma mim-ma ki-a-⸢am⸣ [ša₂] BARti₃ ṣi-i-ti li-ṣi

(o ii 39) If (there is) thus to be anything [of] misfortune, may my loss go out.

o ii 4040

KA.INIM.MA EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈ [DU₃.DU₃]-BI GE₆ tu-šam-ša!(NI.ŠI) GIR₃ MAN-ka KU₅⸢as⸣

(o ii 40) Incantation: to see a decision. Its ritual: You will spend the night; you will block your king's access.1

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

e-nu-ma UN-MEŠ ṣal-la-ma ⸢qu-lu⸣ GARnu e-diš-ši-ka ina UR₃ GUBaz-ma

(r i 1) When people are sleeping and there is silence, you stand all alone on the roof and sprinkle pure water 3 times before the Wagon (and) 3 times before the Eagle; you throw down maṣhatu-flour. When you have thrown down (the flour), you say this. You lie down (and) you will see the decision.

r i 22

A-MEŠ KU₃-MEŠ ana IGI mulMAR.⸢GID₂.DA⸣ 3-šu₂ ana IGI de-ru-⸢u₂⸣-[a] ⸢3⸣-šu₂ tel-liḫ ZI₃.MAD.GA₂

r i 33

ŠUBuk e-nu-ma ŠUBku ⸢an⸣-nam DU₁₁.GA NU₂-⸢ma⸣ EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈

r i 44

<EN₂> dNIN.LIL₂!(E₂) be-let AN⸢e u₃ KI⸣ti₃ AMA DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

(r i 4) Incantation: O Ninlil, the lady of heaven and earth, the mother of the great gods, without whom Ellil, the lord of destinies, cannot determine (destinies). If so-and-so, son of so-and-so, is to achieve his wish, may a star shoot from my right and cross to my left.

r i 55

ša₂ ina ba-li-ša₂ dEN.LIL₂!(E₂) be-⸢el NAM⸣-MEŠ la NAM-MEŠ ki-i NENNI A NENNI

r i 66

AŠ₂-su i-kaš-ša₂-du MUL ⸢TA⸣ ZAG-ia₅ lil-su-ma-am-ma ana GUB₃-ia DIBiq

r i 77

KA.INIM.MA KA.AŠ BAR.BAR.RE DU₃.DU₃-BI ŠE GAL TIqe₂ lu₂TUR ša₂ MUNUS la₃ ZU

(r i 7) Incantation to make a decision. Its ritual: You take full-grown barley; a child who does not know a woman purifies himself on a favourable day. He grinds (the barley) and during the night, the child ignites the censer of burāšu-juniper and this maṣhatu-flour on the roof before the Wagon and stands to the side.

r i 88

ina U₄ ŠE.GA ⸢u₂⸣-ta-lal ⸢i⸣-ṭe₄-en-ma ina GE₆ ina UR₃ ana IGI mulMAR.GID₂.DA

r i 99

NIG₂.NA šim<LI> u ZI₃.MAD.GA₂ an-⸢ni⸣-tu₂ lu₂TUR u₂-nam-mar-ma BAR.BAR GUB.BA

r i 1010

EN₂ an-ni-tu₂ 3-šu₂ ana ⸢IGI⸣ mulMAR.GID₂.DA ŠIDnu A-MEŠ KU₃-MEŠ BALqi₂-ma EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈

(r i 10) You recite this incantation 3 times before the Wagon. You libate pure water. You will see the decision.

r i 1111

EN₂ DINGIR-⸢MEŠ⸣ GAL-MEŠ šu-ut da-⸢nu₃⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ šu-ut d50 DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ šu-ut de₂-a

(r i 11) Incantation: O great gods, those of Anu; O great gods, those of Ellil; O great gods, those of Ea;

r i 1212

šu-ut da-nu₃ al-si-⸢ku-nu-ši⸣ šu-ut d50 na-as-ḫu-ra-ni

(r i 12) I call out to you, those of Anu. Those of Ellil, favour me!

r i 1313

šu-ut de₂-a ⸢GIM dun-qi⸣-ku-nu qu-la-ni

(r i 13) Those of Ea, pay attention to me according to your goodness!

r i 1414

ana-ku NENNI ša₂ a-⸢na⸣ a-[...] lu-ma-ši-ku-nu šit-ku-nu pa-nu-u₂-a

(r i 14) (As for) myself, so-and-so, who is permanently facing ... [...] your constellation:

r i 1515

an-na ⸢GI.NA⸣ ap!(SI-)⸢la-a⸣-nin-ni-ma ina ŠA₃ MUL-MEŠ ANe la₃ ŠID-MEŠ

(r i 15) answer me with a firm yes! From among the countless heavenly stars, glowing, [...], flashing, twinkling, toward your breasts, may a star shoot from my right and cross to my left.

r i 1616

kab-bu-ti [... ṣar]-⸢ḫu!⸣-ti ṣar-ru-ti mi-iḫ-rat GABA-MEŠ-ku-nu

r i 1717

MUL ⸢TA⸣ [ZAG-ia₅ lil]-su-ma-am-ma a-na 2.30-ia₅ DIBiq

r i 1818

⸢šum-ma⸣ [a-na A₂.AŠ₂ TA] ⸢EGIR⸣-ia₅ a-na IGI-ia₅ DIBiq

(r i 18) If [(performed) for a wish], may it cross [from] behind me to in front of me!

r i 1919

⸢KA⸣.[INIM.MA KA.AŠ BAR.BAR].⸢RE⸣ <<DIŠ>> DU₃.DU₃-BI še-am ša 1 ḫar-bi TIqe₂

(r i 19) Incantation [to make a decision]. Its ritual: You take (enough) barley for 1 plough.

r i 2020

⸢ŠE⸣ [...] ḪI IR PAP? IR lu₂TUR ša₂ MUNUS la₃ ZU ARA₃en

(r i 20) The barley [...] .... A child who does not know a woman grinds (the barley).

r i 2121

ana ⸢IGI⸣ [mulMAR.GID₂.DA NIG₂].⸢NA⸣ šimLI GARan GIR₃? ⸢MAN⸣-ka KU₅as

(r i 21) You set up a censer of burāšu-juniper before [the Wagon] (and) block your king's access.2

r i 2222

x [...] ⸢EN₂⸣ 3-šu₂ ana IGI mulMAR.GID₂.DA ŠID-ma

(r i 22) ... [...] you recite the incantation 3 times before the Wagon.

r i 2323

⸢šum⸣-[ma MUL TA ZA₃]-⸢ka⸣ a-na 2.30-ka DIBiq SIG₁₅

(r i 23) If [a star] crosses [from] your [right] to your left - favourable.

r i 2424

⸢šum⸣-[ma MUL TA 2.30]-⸢ka⸣ a-na ZA₃-ka DIBiq la₃ SIG₁₅

(r i 24) If [a star] crosses [from] your [left] to your right - unfavourable.

r i 2525

šum-⸢ma⸣ [MUL TA IGI]-⸢ka⸣ a-na EGIR-ka DIBiq la₃ SIG₁₅

(r i 25) If [a star] crosses [from] your [front] to your rear - unfavourable.

r i 2626

šum-⸢ma⸣ [MUL TA EGIR]-⸢ka⸣ a-na IGI-ka DIBiq SIG₁₅

(r i 26) If [a star] crosses [from] your [rear] to your front - favourable.

r i 2727

šum-⸢ma⸣ [...] x mulMAR.GID₂.DA DIBiq SIG₁₅

(r i 27) If [...] ... passes by the Wagon - favourable.

r i 2828

šum-⸢ma⸣ [...] ⸢GAR⸣-ma ana ŠA₃ mulMAR.GID₂.DA KU₄ SIG₁₅

(r i 28) If [...] is placed and enters into the Wagon - favourable.

r i 2929

EN₂ ⸢al⸣-si-ku-nu-ši DINGIR-MEŠ DI.KU₅-MEŠ ina ANe GAL-MEŠ

(r i 29) Incantation: I call out to you, gods, judges in the great heavens.

r i 3030

ina ⸢su?-pe?⸣-e šu!-ke-ni ak-ta-nar-rab-ku-nu-ši

(r i 30) In supplication and prostration, I constantly pray to you.

r i 3131

[di]-⸢pa⸣-ru ⸢na⸣-mir-tu ša₂ qe₂-reb ša₂-ma-me : ana ⸢nu⸣-ri-ku-nu ṣu-mu-rat mit-ḫar-tu₂

(r i 31) O bright torch that is in the heavens : mankind strives for your light.

r i 3232

[ana] ⸢da⸣-a-nu-ti-ku-nu UN-⸢MEŠ⸣ [i]-qul-lu : ana EŠ.⸢BAR⸣-ku-nu i-kan-nu-uš en-šu

(r i 32) The people pay attention [to] your judicature. : The weak one bows down to your decisions.

r i 3333

DINGIR-MEŠ da-a-a-nu ša₂ ⸢la⸣ BALu₂ qi₂-bit-su-⸢un⸣

(r i 33) O gods, judges whose command cannot be changed, during this night watch I will cause pure underground water to flow on the forehead of an ox.

r i 3434

ina šat mu-ši an-ne₂-e A-MEŠ IDIM KU₃-MEŠ uš-te-še-ra ana pu-ut ⸢GU₄!⸣

r i 3535

DI-ku-nu ki-na u EŠ.BAR DINGIR⸢ti⸣-ku-nu GALti lu-mur-ma qa-ba-a lu-⸢uš⸣-kun

(r i 35) Let me see your firm judgement and the decision of your great divinity, so that I may establish the prognosis!

r i 3636

šum-ma NENNI A NENNI AŠ₂-su i-kaš-ša₂-du GU₄ EŠ.BAR lid-di-na TU₆ EN₂

(r i 36) If so-and-so, son of so-and-so, is to achieve his wish, let the ox give the decision. Incantation formula.

r i 3737

DU₃.DU₃-BI ina U₄ ŠE.GA tal-ta-ad-da-ad dugLA.ḪA.AN ⸢u₃?⸣ x MU TIqe₂

(r i 37) Its ritual: On a favourable day, you drag yourself (to a ritual site). You take a lahannu-flask and ...

r i 3838

lu₂TUR ša₂ MUNUS la₃ ZUu ina ID₂ A-MEŠ i-sab-ba NIG₂.NA šimLI u ZI₃.MAD.GA₂

(r i 38) A child who does not know a woman draws water from the river. You strew a censer of burāšu-juniper and some maṣhatu-flour before the gods of the night; you libate first-class beer; you lift up that water and recite the incantation 3 times. You libate (the water) 3 times on the forehead of the lying ox and you will see the decision.

Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

ana IGI DINGIR-MEŠ GE₆ti₃ DUB!(UM)aq KAŠ SAG BALqi₂ A-MEŠ šu-nu-te IL₂-ma

r ii 22

EN₂ 3-šu₂ ŠIDnu ana pu-ut GU₄ rab-ṣi 3-šu₂ BALqi₂-ma EŠ.BAR IGI.DU₈

r ii 33

šum₄-ma GU₄ is-su-us-ma it-bi KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 3) If the ox lows and gets up: achievement of wish.

r ii 44

šum₄-ma GU₄ is-su-us-ma la₃ it-bi la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 4) If the ox lows and does not get up: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 55

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma TE-su ana 15-šu₂ ŠUB <<ina>> la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 5) If the ox gets up and inclines its head to the right: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 66

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma TE-su ana 2.30-šu₂ ŠUB <<ina>> KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 6) If the ox gets up and inclines its head to the left: achievement of wish.

r ii 77

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZU₂-MEŠ-šu₂ im-ruq-ma it-bi <<ina>> KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 7) If the ox grinds its teeth and gets up: achievement of wish.

r ii 88

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZU₂-MEŠ-šu₂ im-ruq-ma la₃ it-bi <<ina>> la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 8) If the ox grinds its teeth and does not get up: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 99

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma ana IGI-šu₂ DU <<ina>> KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 9) If the ox gets up and goes forwards: achievement of wish.

r ii 1010

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma ana EGIR-šu₂ DU <<ina>> la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 10) If the ox gets up and goes backwards: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 1111

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma ana ZA₃-šu₂ DU <<ina>> la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 11) If the ox gets up and goes to its right: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 1212

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma ana 2.30-šu₂ DU KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 12) If the ox gets up and goes to its left: achievement of wish.

r ii 1313

⸢šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI⸣-ma is-si KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 13) If the ox gets up and bellows: achievement of wish.

r ii 1414

[šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI]-⸢ma⸣ ḫu-ru-up-pa-šu₂ LA₂qi₂ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 14) [If the ox gets up] and raises its pelvis: achievement of wish.

r ii 1515

[šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI]-⸢ma⸣ ḫu-ru-up-pa-šu₂ uš-ta-pil₂ šu-uz-zu-⸢qu⸣ [la₃] KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 15) [If the ox gets up] and lowers its pelvis: upset, [non]-achievement of wish.

r ii 1616

[šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI]-⸢ma⸣ SAḪAR-ḪI.A ana UGU-šu₂ is-lu la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 16) [If the ox gets up] and stirs up dust in front of it: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 1717

[šum₄-ma] ⸢GU₄ ZI-ma⸣ ina SI ZA₃-šu₂ SAḪAR-ḪI.A is-lu KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 17) [If] the ox gets up and stirs up dust with its right horn: achievement of wish.

r ii 1818

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma ina SI 2.30-šu₂ SAḪAR-ḪI.A is-lu la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 18) If the ox gets up and stirs up dust with its left horn: non-achievement of wish.

r ii 1919

šum₄-ma GU₄ ZI-ma ina GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ maḫ-⸢ra⸣-a-ti ⸢SAḪAR⸣-ḪI.A ana EGIR-šu₂ is-lu la₃ KURad AŠ₂

(r ii 19) If the ox gets up and stirs up dust behind itself with its front feet: non-achievement of wish.

(colophon)
r ii 2020

GABA.RI E₂.SA.BAD ša₃-⸢ṭir₂ md⸣PA!(AŠ₂—)šab-ši lu₂ŠAMAN₂!(DUGUD)LA₂ TUR

(r ii 20) Copy of Esabad. Writer: Nabu-šabši, the junior scribal apprentice, son of Nabu-eriba, scribe.

r ii 2121

DUMU mdPA—SU lu₂A.BA

1 Uncertain reading . Based on the context , E . Reiner emended the end of this line to arkatka taparras "you will find out your future " (1960 : 34 n . 22).

2 Cf . obv . ii 40.

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2011 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338390
